title: Ben-Gvir's video of European activists exhibits his policy of ill-treating Palestinian prisoners
subhead: Israel's far-right National Security Minister sparked international outcry and was even rebuked by the US ambassador and Benjamin Netanyahu after he posted footage of European activists held with heads to the ground and hands bound.

title: 'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique

Trump tightens grip on Republican Party in US primaries
subhead: Candidates backed by Donald Trump for the Republican Party nomination in the November midterm elections benefit from the mobilization of the MAGA base, which remains loyal to the billionaire despite his declining popularity.

title: Xi, at center of global affairs, keeps Putin waiting on Power of Siberia 2 pipeline
subhead: Vladimir Putin, who, after Donald Trump, came to Beijing to celebrate the 25th anniversary of the China-Russia friendship treaty, failed to secure the firm commitment he had hoped for from Xi Jinping regarding the pipeline, which is intended to carry gas from northwestern Russia to China.

title: Who is Emmanuel Moulin, the new governor of France's central bank?
subhead: Parliament approved the appointment of the former Elysée chief of staff to head the Banque de France on Wednesday, May 20. Opponents criticized his closeness to both Nicolas Sarkozy and Emmanuel Macron.

title: JR covers Pont-Neuf bridge in Paris with giant inflatable cave
subhead: The temporary installation 'La Caverne du Pont-Neuf' has been set up on the Paris landmark since Thursday. The site will be open to the public free of charge, 24 hours a day, for three weeks from June 6.

Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
subhead: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

title: Cuba's crackdown on dissidents continues, despite US pressure
subhead: In an effort to show good faith to Washington, Havana recently pardoned more than 2,000 detainees. However, most of those who benefited were ordinary delinquents. The Cuban authorities continue to arrest dissidents, bringing the number of political prisoners to 1,260, a record high.

title: How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
subhead: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.
